commit.message:
Fix crossed book: snapshot catch-up MIN, diagnostic stats

Root cause: TrackSnapshotHeader used MAX(LastMsgSeqNumProcessed)
across instruments instead of MIN. Instruments captured early in the
snapshot cycle lost incrementals between MIN and MAX, causing phantom
orders and persistently crossed books.

Fix: use MIN so all instruments receive all incrementals after the
earliest snapshot capture point. Redundant replays for later-captured
instruments are handled gracefully by existing order logic.

Additions:
- /stats endpoint: order counters, gap stats, crossed book list
- BookManager diagnostic counters (adds/updates/deletes/deleteNotFound)
- ChannelHandler counters (processed/duplicates/gaps)
- Crossed-book transition detection with logging (first 20)
- ChannelHandlerProvider wired in Program.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add script to migrate legacy JSONL raw-layer tables to Iceberg

Legacy tables in ol_warehouse_{env}_raw were written as newline-delimited JSON
by Airbyte before the platform standardized on Iceberg format via dlt.  This
script converts those tables so they are accessible via the StarRocks
ol_data_lake_iceberg external catalog.

Key safety features:
- dry-run by default (--no-dry-run required to make changes)
- Positive eligibility check: only migrates TextInputFormat + JSON SerDe tables
- Saves original Glue table definition and restores it if Iceberg creation fails
- Validates row count from Iceberg snapshot after write
- Uses pa.fs.S3FileSystem (PyArrow-native) for reliable dataset reads
- Warns about non-Iceberg, non-JSONL tables without touching them

Usage:
  uv run python bin/migrate-raw-jsonl-to-iceberg.py --env qa
  uv run python bin/migrate-raw-jsonl-to-iceberg.py --env qa --no-dry-run

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix excess spike indices in CurationV1/SpikeSorting get_sorting by removing invalid spikes (#1564)

* Initial plan

* Fix invalid spike_times in CurationV1 by clipping out-of-bounds indices

When spike times (stored in seconds) are converted back to sample indices
using np.searchsorted, floating-point precision issues can cause the
returned index to equal n_samples (one past the end), which SpikeInterface
rejects with a ValueError. This fix clips the indices to [0, n_samples-1]
and logs a warning when this occurs, in both CurationV1.get_sorting() and
SpikeSorting.get_sorting().

Fixes #1092

fix: Update enable_auto_tracing defaults for hosted agent compatibility (#474)

## Summary

Updates enable_auto_tracing() so a bare call works correctly with Azure
AI Foundry hosted agents and also covers LangGraph callback-manager
construction paths used by invoke() and ainvoke().

## Changes

| Area | Before | After | Reason |
|------|--------|-------|--------|
| enable_content_recording default | True | False | Content recording
should be opt-in for privacy and security |
| provider_name default | None | azure.ai.openai | Uses the canonical
GenAI provider name for Azure OpenAI spans |
| auto_configure_azure_monitor default | True | False | Hosted agents
configure their own TracerProvider; calling configure_azure_monitor()
can create conflicting providers and duplicate or lost spans |
| LangGraph callback-manager hooks | BaseCallbackManager.__init__ only |
BaseCallbackManager.__init__ plus LangGraph callback-manager helper
factories | Ensures tracer injection also covers LangGraph helper paths
used by invoke() and ainvoke() |

Note: message_keys is unchanged. The tracer already defaults to
[messages] when it is not explicitly provided.

## Additional updates

- normalize azure_openai and azure-openai aliases to azure.ai.openai
before assigning the default provider name
- patch LangGraph callback-manager helper functions in
langgraph._internal._config, langgraph._internal._runnable, and
langgraph.pregel.main so the tracer is injected into managers created
for graph execution
- add regression tests covering the new default resolution path and the
LangGraph async callback-manager helper patch
- clarify in the docstring that non-hosted apps must pass
auto_configure_azure_monitor=True or configure telemetry themselves

## Motivation

Hosted agent samples previously had to pass explicit parameters to avoid
conflicts, especially auto_configure_azure_monitor=False. In addition,
relying only on BaseCallbackManager.__init__ is brittle when LangGraph
builds callback managers through helper functions on the invoke and
ainvoke paths.

After this change, enable_auto_tracing() with no arguments works better
in hosted environments and explicitly patches the LangGraph
callback-manager factory path as well. In non-hosted scripts or
notebooks, either pass auto_configure_azure_monitor=True or configure a
TracerProvider and exporter separately.

## Backward Compatibility

All parameters still accept explicit values. Environment variable
overrides still work. The behavioral change is limited to the defaults
used when parameters and relevant environment variables are not
supplied, plus broader tracer injection coverage for LangGraph
callback-manager construction paths.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(issue-triage): improve agentic workflow prompt and fix label application (#4115)

* fix(issue-triage): use add-labels safe output for label application

The issue triage workflow was using update-issue to apply labels, but
the update_issue tool description only mentions 'status, title, or body'
- it does not mention labels. The agent therefore never called it for
label application, resulting in labels being mentioned in the triage
comment but never actually applied to the issue.

Fix:
- Add add-labels safe output (max: 10) to the frontmatter
- Update Critical Label Rules to use add-labels instead of update-issue
- Update Safe Outputs section with explicit combined case: call both
  add-labels AND add-comment (the most common scenario), with a clear
  warning that listing labels in the comment body alone does NOT apply them

Recompiled issue-triage.lock.yml to include the new add_labels handler.

commit.message:
Fix bazaar scraper retry policy causing alt finder search timeouts (#26)

Alt finder searches were consistently hitting the 25s API timeout,
returning "Backend timed out" to the user. The culprit was
`BazaarScraperHttp4sClient`'s retry policy retrying on **all** failures
— including network timeouts — up to 2 times. With a 5s per-request
timeout, a single timed-out bazaar call stalled for 17s (5+1+5+1+5s).
Since `findAndPrintAlts` calls the scraper twice sequentially (initial
trade-date lookup + `enrichWithCandidateTrades` for up to 20
candidates), the budget was trivially blown.

## Changes

- **`BazaarScraperHttp4sClient`**: Restrict retries to HTTP 5xx only; do
not retry on timeouts (`Left[Throwable]`) or 4xx (including 429, already
handled by cooldown). Cap at 1 retry via named `maxRetries` constant.

```scala
// Before — retried on everything, including timeouts
private val retryPolicy: RetryPolicy[IO] = (_, result, unsuccessfulAttempts) => {
  if unsuccessfulAttempts > 2 then None
  else if result.exists(_.status == Status.Ok) then None
  else 1.second.some
}

// After — timeouts (Left) and 4xx fall through to None immediately
private val maxRetries = 1
private val retryPolicy: RetryPolicy[IO] = (_, result, unsuccessfulAttempts) => {
  if unsuccessfulAttempts >= maxRetries then None
  else result match {
    case Right(resp) if resp.status.code >= 500 => 1.second.some
    case _ => None
  }
}
```

Worst-case latency per bazaar call drops from **17s → 5s**, keeping the
full search path well within the 25s budget.
